correctnessRule: an arm may not lose a task solved by baseline
commonPrompt: Route by task shape. For a tiny single-file change, use native tools even if AIShell exists. For repeated or multi-file workspace observation, you must start with workspace_snapshot when it exists and use its embedded context before drilldown. For a repository check expected to emit more than 32 KiB, you must use run_check when it exists; use artifact_read only if its primary diagnostic is insufficient. If those tools do not exist, use the native tools available to you.

tasks.prompt: Fix the implementation so the repository tests pass. Inspect only what is needed, make the smallest correct edit, and run the deterministic test before finishing.

Diagnose and fix the syntax failure, then run the repository check defined by package.json. The check intentionally emits high-volume dependency diagnostics before the primary error. Preserve the intended exported greeting and avoid unrelated edits.
Update the runtime protocol version from 1 to 2 everywhere required by the tests. Discover the relevant files, make only necessary edits, and run the deterministic test.

Do not edit files. You must call these AIShell MCP tools exactly once each: workspace_snapshot; read_context for probe.txt; search_context for the fixed string needle; run_check with /usr/bin/printf and arguments [%s, probe]; then artifact_read using the returned stdout handle. Finish only after all five calls succeed.
